description: PayPal Pay Later button integration for US — allows buyers to pay in installments (Pay in 4 or Pay Monthly)

script_parameters.client-id: Required. Your PayPal REST client ID
script_parameters.enable-funding: paylater — explicitly enables the Pay Later button
script_parameters.disable-funding: paylater — to disable the Pay Later button
script_parameters.currency: USD — required for US Pay Later
script_parameters.components: buttons — default, includes Pay Later when enabled

callbacks.createOrder: function(data, actions) { return actions.order.create({ purchase_units: [{ amount: { value: '100.00' } }] }); }

all_buttons_with_paylater.note: When enable-funding=paylater is in the script tag, Pay Later button renders automatically alongside PayPal button

clientId.code: const sdkInstance = await window.paypal.createInstance({ clientId: 'YOUR_CLIENT_ID', components: ['paypal-payments'], pageType: 'checkout' });
clientId.note: Recommended for most integrations. No server-side token needed.

clientToken.code: const sdkInstance = await window.paypal.createInstance({ clientToken, components: ['paypal-payments'], pageType: 'checkout' });
clientToken.note: Required for PayPal vaulting and Fastlane.

initialization.note: Pay Later is included in the paypal-payments component — no separate component needed

createOrder_return.note: v6 requires returning an object with orderId. This differs from v5 which returned just the order ID string.

order_creation.note: No special payload fields needed for Pay Later. Standard order creation works. PayPal determines Pay Later eligibility at checkout based on order amount, buyer location, and buyer credit profile.

eligibility.v5: Automatic — button shows if eligible based on script params
eligibility.v6: Explicit — must call findEligibleMethods() and check isEligible('paylater')

merchant: US-based PayPal merchant with Business Account
One-time payment integration (NOT recurring or reference transactions)
buyer: Order amount within range ($30–$1,500 for Pay in 4; $49–$10,000 for Pay Monthly)

best_practices: Always check eligibility before showing Pay Later button (v6)
Extract and set productCode and countryCode from getDetails() (v6)
Provide standard PayPal as fallback when Pay Later is not eligible
Do not promise Pay Later availability before eligibility is confirmed
Use enable-funding=paylater in script tag (v5) to ensure button appears
Keep order creation and amount validation on the server side
Test with sandbox accounts using eligible US buyer and amounts in range
